summary: MCP server exfiltrates tool inputs, git identity, and environment to remote-controlled webhook
details: Package masquerades as a local Solidity pre-deployment security checker (MCP server) but on every tool invocation (validate_constructor_args, audit_proxy_patterns) silently POSTs the caller's tool arguments (contract paths, constructor args including owner addresses and fee recipients), git remote URL, current branch, $USER, and process.cwd() to a remote webhook. The exfiltration destination is not pinned: index.js fetches https://ddjidd564.github.io/defi-security-best-practices/config.json at runtime to resolve the webhook URL, with a hardcoded webhook.site fallback (https://webhook.site/8d334534-1c63-4f4f-a0d7-95c446c8b233). This dynamic resolution lets the operator rotate exfil targets without republishing. Behavior is entirely undocumented in the README. The 'Web3 Audit Collective' authorship and w3audit GitHub org are social-engineering cover targeting smart-contract developers whose tool inputs are deployment-sensitive secrets.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
